agenda_briefs.title: Dennis Back appointed Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 general local election, scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. The previous appointee, Samantha Pellizzari, is on leave and not expected to return before the election. Alix Nay and Josh Comia remain as Deputy Chief Election Officers.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby general local election date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
1.
CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

attachments.title: CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal blocked — petition sufficient
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a Certificate of Sufficiency confirming that petitions against the proposed North Road BIA renewal bylaw met the legal threshold: owners of at least 50% of parcels and 50% of assessed value opposed. The report advises Council not to proceed with adoption. The BIA would have run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031, with levies on Class 5 (Light Industrial) and Class 6 (Business) properties.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed — the renewal will not proceed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

see Bylaw #14797

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 corporate maintenance: KPMG auditor, Dec 12 reference date
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving routine annual corporate maintenance for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026. Items include selecting December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, appointing KPMG as auditor until the next AGM, receiving the BHA's financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2025, and appointing directors for two- and three-year terms. The decision is pending at the April 14, 2026 council meeting.

key_dates.label: Council vote on BHA 2026 corporate maintenance matters
BHA annual reference date selected

agenda_briefs.source_body:

Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA).

agenda_briefs.title: BHA 2026 budget and 2025 financials: $2M city funding contingent on council receipt
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and its proposed 2026 operating budget. The BHA reported a clean audit opinion from KPMG, an accumulated surplus of $7.52M, and cash holdings of $31.75M as of Dec 31, 2025. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00M in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1. Council has not yet approved the 2026 budget — this is a presentation for discussion and a future vote.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share pilot: parking zones, speed rules, operator fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to three bylaws to launch an e-bike share pilot and address the provincial e-scooter pilot. Key changes: shared e-bikes and e-scooters must park only in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; operators need a City agreement; e-scooters are banned on major road networks and arterials except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; riding on sidewalks is prohibited unless signed otherwise; headphones over both ears are banned while riding; and violations in parks (e.g. riding outside designated areas) will be enforced under the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, businesses with property near proposed parking zones or that operate shared micromobility fleets must comply with new parking, speed, and operator agreement rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council decision on e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby housing target progress report: 910 net new units, 10,240 target by 2030
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on forwarding a 6-month progress report showing 910 net new housing units completed (1,097 completions minus 187 demolitions) against a provincial target of 10,240 net new units by August 31, 2030. The report notes a provincial pause on Community Housing Fund programs is creating uncertainty for affordable projects like Hall Towers (331 units in Edmonds).

key_dates.label: Burnaby housing target Year 1b report deadline (Sep 1, 2025 – Aug 31, 2026)
Burnaby housing target Year 2 report deadline (Sep 1, 2026 – Aug 31, 2027)
Burnaby housing target Year 3 report deadline (Sep 1, 2027 – Aug 31, 2028)
Burnaby housing target Year 4 report deadline (Sep 1, 2028 – Aug 31, 2029)
Burnaby housing target Year 5 final deadline (Sep 1, 2029 – Aug 31, 2030)

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 — no new fees for owners
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the voluntary Building Benchmark BC program. If approved, the city would share energy data from enrolled buildings (including 30 civic buildings) with the province. The program is free for participating building owners and covers buildings of all sizes — over 1,900 buildings and 15 million m² are already enrolled province-wide. No additional funding is required beyond the existing 2026 budget.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. If approved, building owners may be recruited to join the free program to receive energy-use and emissions benchmarking data.

agenda_briefs.title: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain for Mintara restaurant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use specific land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, including the Mintara restaurant. The City is not requesting compensation for the right-of-way.

key_dates.label: Council decision on FortisBC right-of-way — Burnaby

agenda_briefs.industries: Natural Resources, Construction & Development, Food Service

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular housing four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey building will include four affordable rental units (one accessible) with 5,319 sq ft of gross floor area and private outdoor space for each unit. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. The City will retain ownership and lease the units as part of its residential rental portfolio.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, this pilot could lead to more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Council vote on modular housing pilot contract award

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The project includes installing 45 lineal meters of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal meters of DI watermain, and replacing Metro Vancouver sanitary main. Funding comes from the Canada Community Building Fund Reserve and DCC Drainage Reserve Fund.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. The project replaces 16 culverts between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road, including major replacements at Beecher Creek and Crab Apple Creek, plus final paving of the trail. Funding includes TransLink contributions for the roads component.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $450K contract change for Rogers cellular services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a $450,000 contract change (including taxes) to continue cellular voice and data services with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. on a month-to-month basis for up to nine months while the city transitions to a new provider. The total estimated contract value is $3,495,900. The change is needed to avoid service disruption during the transition.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Rogers cellular contract change

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for HVAC maintenance at city buildings
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving a contract change of $579,600 (including tax) to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC system maintenance and repair services at various City-owned buildings. The change is needed to continue services while the City goes back to market for competitive pricing. The estimated total contract value is now $5,412,750 including tax.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx contact centre licence renewal
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a three-year contract renewal with Long View Systems Corporation for Cisco WebEx Contact Center license subscriptions, estimated at $394,942 including taxes. The renewal covers May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029, and is needed to maintain the city's customer service centre phone system and support future call queue migrations.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection contract renewal to A.W Fireguard
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on renewing a three-year contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for fire protection systems and equipment installation, maintenance, and repair services at city-owned buildings. agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque wording approved for April 26, 2026 opening
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the exact wording and design for a commemorative plaque to be installed on the new Burnaby Lake Overpass. The plaque will mark the official opening of the overpass on April 26, 2026. The plaque text lists the Mayor, Councillors, Chief Administrative Officer, Owner's Engineer (McElhanney), Contractor (Jacob Bros Construction Inc.), and acknowledges partnership funding from TransLink, Government of Canada, and Province of British Columbia.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening and plaque unveiling

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby council to vote on external financial reviewer to curb tax hikes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on hiring an external reviewer to assess the city's financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priorities. The review aims to safeguard assets and mitigate risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents, driven by a sharp decline in density bonus revenues and higher procurement costs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider contacting your councillor to share your views on this proposal.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES
1.
FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

attachments.title: FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building with 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom) at 5035 Beresford Street to First and Second Reading. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial District and would change to a Comprehensive Development District. The proposal includes 38 residential vehicle parking spaces (100% EV ready), 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 574.74 m² of outdoor rooftop amenity area. Prerequisites include a 1.9 m road dedication along Beresford Street, a Housing Agreement for non-market rental units, and connection to the City's District Energy Utility system when available.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.

key_dates.label: Council vote to forward rezoning to First and Second Reading

agenda_briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth update received
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through a business community survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13 to April 2, 2026. Formal recommendations will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No decisions or new rules were voted on.

key_dates.label: Task Force recommendations presented to Council

agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a report that Oxford Properties forfeited a $1,363,500 security deposit instead of building the public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court, part of the Riverbend Business Park rezoning. The City now holds the funds and may use them for the pier or an alternative public amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby. The covenant will be discharged from title once the forfeited security is received.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
1.
Amenity Pier Covenant  Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw up for final adoption — 5-year levy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would create a new Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road for a 5-year term (April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2031). The BIA would levy a local service tax on properties classified as Class 5 (light industry) or Class 6 (business/other) based on assessed value, raising up to $1,755,147.81 total ($317,954.94 in Year 1). Enough property owners have submitted formal petitions against the proposal, so the outcome is uncertain.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the bylaw passes, your property may be subject to new BIA fees based on assessed value.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA term begins
Burnaby North Road BIA term ends
